senses.definition: to pass the time without doing anything important or productive

examples.x: He admitted to sometimes beating off by simply staring out the window.
The tendency to beat off by browsing social media should be minimized.
The habit of beating off during work hours can affect productivity.
The tendency to beat off by browsing social media should be minimized.

senses.definition: to cancel what has been planned

examples.x: We might need to call off the picnic if it keeps raining.
The event was called off at the last minute due to low attendance.
The authorities had to call off the festival due to security concerns.

senses.definition: to put a check mark on or near an item to show it is done or verified

examples.x: They checked off the completed steps on the project timeline.
I checked off the groceries on the shopping list.
The teacher asked us to check off our names on the attendance sheet.

senses.definition: to mark an item or task on a list as completed or canceled by drawing a line through it

examples.x: As the team accomplished milestones, they would cross off tasks on the project timeline.
The manager instructed the assistant to cross off the irrelevant items from the agenda.

senses.definition: to cancel a commitment or obligation, often at the last minute, by providing an excuse

examples.x: Despite promising to attend the party, she had to cry off due to a sudden work emergency.
He regretfully cried off from the hiking trip, citing health issues that arose unexpectedly.
The team captain had to cry off the tournament due to an unexpected family emergency.
Mary had to cry off from the meeting as her child fell ill and needed immediate attention.
Tom had planned to join the charity event but had to cry off because his car broke down.

senses.definition: to complete or finalize something, especially in a successful or satisfying manner

examples.x: The chef used fresh herbs to finish off the dish and make it perfect.
They were able to finish off the project ahead of the deadline.
I'll finish off the report and send it to the client for review.

senses.definition: to conclude or cease, often in an abrupt or incomplete manner

examples.x: The story leaves off with the protagonist facing an uncertain future, leaving readers eager for the sequel.
The meeting left off with a sense of urgency, as the team was tasked with completing a critical project by the end of the week.
The scientist's groundbreaking research left off with a promising discovery, paving the way for future advancements.
The game left off in a tense moment, leaving fans eagerly awaiting the next match.
After weeks of practice, the team polished off their final rehearsal flawlessly.
With determination, he polished off the last of his exams.
She polished off her assignments well before the deadline.
Even with distractions, he managed to polish off his thesis by the end of the month.

senses.definition: to avoid dealing with something, such as a responsibility or an issue

examples.x: She keeps putting off the decision, hoping the problem will resolve itself.

senses.definition: to cancel or postpone a match or game due to heavy rain or unfavorable weather conditions

examples.x: The soccer game was rained off because of the torrential downpour.
The tennis match had to be rained off due to the thunderstorm.
They decided to rain off the cricket match as the field was soaked.
Unfortunately, the baseball game was rained off and rescheduled for next week.
The track and field event had to be rained off for safety reasons during the lightning storm.
She had to ring off quickly because she was running late.
Please ring off the call when you're done discussing the details.
I'll ring off now, but let's continue our conversation later.
He rang off abruptly, leaving the conversation unresolved.
The customer service representative rang off after providing the requested information.

senses.definition: to conclude an event or activity in a satisfying manner

examples.x: The conference rounded off with a networking session for participants.
The meeting rounded off on a positive note with constructive feedback.
The event rounded off smoothly, thanks to the efficient organization.
The novel rounded off with a satisfying and conclusive ending.
Let's round off the workshop with a brief reflection on what we've learned.

senses.definition: to write the final message at the end of the letter or email that counts as one's signature

examples.x: After sharing the latest news, Mary signed off her email with warm regards and well-wishes.
John concluded his letter by signing off with a friendly note and his signature.
As the email exchange wrapped up, Sarah signed off with a casual "Take care!"
In the closing paragraph, David signed off his handwritten letter with a personal touch, expressing gratitude for the recipient's friendship.
The pen pals had developed a routine of signing off each letter with a unique and shared closing phrase that held sentimental value.
